oatmeal-ice-cream-cookie-sandwiches.name: Oatmeal Ice Cream Cookie Sandwiches
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Cook butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat, swirling often, until it foams, then browns, 5–8 minutes. Scrape brown butter and toasty solids into a heatproof measuring glass and let cool.

Process waffle cones, oats, flour, and salt in a food processor in long pulses until oats are broken up and cones are finely ground. Add brown sugar and pulse to combine. Whisk egg yolk, vanilla, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl. With the motor running, slowly drizzle into food processor, then stream in brown butter; process until dough forms a ball around the blade.

Portion out slightly rounded tablespoonfuls of dough to make 26 balls (each should weigh about ¾ oz.). Roll balls between your palms to smooth and divide between 2 parchment-lined rimmed baking sheets, spacing 3" apart (the cookies will spread as they bake). Cover with another sheet of parchment paper and use the bottom of a glass to flatten each into a 2½" round.

Bake cookies, rotating sheets front to back and top to bottom halfway through, until golden all over and browned around the edges, 12–15 minutes. Let cool on baking sheets; use a thin metal spatula to loosen cookies from parchment. Turn half of cookies over (you’ll have 2 extra).

Remove 2 pints of ice cream (same flavor) from containers (just peel away container if it’s paper, or use a rubber spatula to help slide ice cream out intact if container is plastic). Cut ice cream into 2” pieces and place in a large bowl. Let sit until slightly softened, about 5 minutes, then stir with a wooden spoon or a large rubber spatula until the consistency of thick cake batter.

Using a 4-oz. ice cream scoop or ½-cup measuring cup, and working one at a time or in small batches, scoop out large domes of ice cream onto upside-down cookies. Top each with a right-side-up cookie, then press down gently until ice cream extends to the edges. Transfer sandwiches to a rimmed baking sheet set in the freezer as you work. Repeat softening process with remaining 2 pints of ice cream and assemble remaining sandwiches.
Freeze sandwiches until ice cream is hard, about 2 hours. Cover tray with plastic wrap and freeze at least another 8 hours to let cookies soften.
Do Ahead: Cookies can be baked 1 week ahead; transfer to a resealable plastic freezer bag and freeze. Sandwiches can be assembled 5 days ahead; keep frozen.

okra-dish-with-olive-oil-lezzet.name: Okra Dish With Olive Oil
instructions: 1. Clean the stems of Bamians with a sharp bçakla, cut to the cone leg.
2. Then wash in plenty of water and strain.
3. Finely shore onions and garlic.
4. Relate the shells of tomatoes after peeling.
5. In the i&ccedil of the pot, fry the olive oil.
6. By adding onions and yellowms to the car, roast to pink.
7. Then look at the salç until the breadth and reindeed stones.
8. When you start to keep your meal, the mixture is poured in it.
9. After adding hot water and salt, close the cover of the pot.
10. Bake about 20 minutes in medium fire.
11. 11. When the mammals begin to soften, add lemon juice and powder sugar.
12. After cooking for 5 minutes, take it from fire and leave it cool.
13.  Afiyet olsun.

okra-oilers-argiro.name: Okra Oilers
instructions: Clean the okra with a small sharp knife and remove the casing around the head as if we peel them conically. We make sure that their little head stays on top of the okra and doesn't open or rip.
After we're done, we wash them and drain them well.
Put them in a baking pan, pour them with vinegar and sprinkle them with the thick salt. Mix them lightly so the vinegar goes everywhere.
Let them stand for about an hour. If it's summer, we put them in the strong sun.

Why do I marinate them in vinegar and salt? This marinating especially under the hot summer sun will help them stay whole and not run the sticky liquid from inside and their seeds come out in cooking. The result? In cooking they will be sweeter with percussion flesh.

old-fashioned-rhubarb-pie.name: Old-fashioned Rhubarb Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 232°C (220 degrees C).
Arrange pie crust in a 9-inch pie pan. Spread rhubarb in an even layer in the bottom of the crust.
Whisk egg, white sugar, sour cream, and 1/3 cup of flour together in a medium bowl until smooth. Pour over rhubarb.
Mix 1/2 cup of flour and brown sugar together in a small bowl; stir in melted butter until mixture is crumbly. Sprinkle over top of the pie.

Bake for 15 minutes in the preheated oven, then reduce the heat to 177°C (175 degrees C). Continue to bake until the edges have puffed, and the topping is golden, about 40 minutes. The center may still be slightly jiggly. Cool completely before slicing and serving.

omelet-argiro.name: Omelet
instructions: Break the eggs in a bowl and beat them very well, so that they are fully joined.
Add some salt and pepper.
Heat the pan over medium heat and add olive oil and butter.
Once we put the eggs in the pan with a fork, pull the ingredients from the outside to the inside to drill holes in the mixture.
It slowly begins to stabilize and dry its surface.
If a little liquid egg is left on top, spread it on the surface to dry.
The whole processes lasts about 3′.
Once the surface is dry, sprinkle with cheese on half the side.
Tilt the pan a bit and fold the omelet in 2. With the heat in the heart melts the cheese and becomes chewy.
Remove from the fire and place it on a plate.
Some bread and feta are necessary in her serving.

omelette-stuffed-with-tuna-pequerecetas.name: Omelette Stuffed With Tuna
instructions: First we make the omelette, cutting the potatoes and pounding them and frying them as we have custom (if you have Thermomix you can see how it's done here) Then we remove the frying oil and add the scrambling eggs with a chorrit of milk that will make them more spongy. We put them back and put them back.

Once tibia, we cut her in half and filled her up. To fill up, we mix the leaked tuna with the mayonnaise and put about the lower half of the omelette. We cut the lettuce and put it onto tuna. We covered with the other half of the omelette. And ready!

one-pot-cilantro-lime-chicken-rice.name: One-pot Cilantro Lime Chicken & Rice
instructions: In a skillet, heat a small amount of oil on medium–high heat. Add chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the chicken thoroughly, about 6-8 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add remaining olive oil to the skillet. Add onion and peppers and sauté for 5-7 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken broth, lime juice, black beans, tomatoes, rice, salt, and pepper and stir to combine.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until rice is cooked through.
Add chicken back to the pot. Add cilantro and combine.
Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

one-pot-clambake.name: One-pot Clambake

ingredients.prep: kielbasa, or andouille), cut into 2" pieces

instructions: Place a steamer basket in 30-quart pot. Add wine and 12 cups water; cover and bring to a boil. Add potatoes; cover and cook 5 minutes. Add lobsters and eggs; cover and cook 10 minutes. Gently nestle corn and next 6 ingredients (and littlenecks, if using) in pot. Cover and cook 5 minutes. Add Manila clams or steamers (if using), cover, and cook 10 minutes. Add mussels, cover, and cook until shellfish open, about 5 minutes (discard any that do not open). Peel 1 egg and cut in half. If it's hard-boiled, lobsters are ready.

Using a slotted spoon and tongs, transfer clambake to a very large platter or a table covered with newspaper. Sprinkle with spice mix. Pour broth from pot into small bowls, leaving any sediment behind. Serve clambake with broth and melted butter.

one-pot-ground-beef-stroganoff.name: One-pot Ground Beef Stroganoff
instructions: In a 6-quart (5 L) Dutch oven, heat olive oil. Add mushrooms and season with salt and pepper.
Add the onions and garlic. Cook until onions are translucent.
Add the ground beef and cook until brown. Stir in the paprika.
Add beef stock and egg noodles. Give it a stir and cover. Simmer for 15-20 minutes. Lower the heat.
Add the sour cream and cornstarch slurry. Stir until thickened.
Serve and garnish with parsley.
Enjoy!

orange-cookies-argiro.name: Orange Cookies
instructions: Make the cinnamon juice first, boiling 2 cinnamon sticks in water for 5 minutes.
Keep half a cup of cinnamon juice and let it cool down.
Place the sesame in a pan over low heat and roast for a few minutes until golden and allow it to cool.
Shake the flour (1,200 gr) with baking powder.
Beat the sugar with oil with wire or mixer for 5 minutes to whiteen the mixture.
Dissolve the soda in the juice and add them to the olive oil.
Add the cinnamon juice, tsipouro and flour.
First add 1 kilo flour and the rest a little – a little while while, as long as it takes (about 1,200 g flour in total).
We make them and dip them in the sesame.
Add the orange cookies to the sheet and baking for 30-35 minutes in a well preheated evening at 1603

orange-salmon-pequerecetas.name: Orange Salmon

ingredients.item: 350 ml de de zumo de naranja
200 ml of liquid cream for cooking or evaporate milk

instructions: To prepare this salmon with an orange sauce, first of all we have to do the salmon paddles, back and back till they are golden. Let's have a reservation.

In the same frying pan we add some extra virgin olive oil and add a minced leek with some salt. When the leek starts to get golden we put the orange juice, sugar and vinegar, we let cook about ten minutes At that time we put back salmon and cream, leave a few minutes to reduce the salsa a little and are ready to serve.

orecchiette-with-corn-greens-and-ricotta.name: Orecchiette With Corn, Greens, And Ricotta

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Cook orecchiette; drain, reserving ¾ cup pasta cooking liquid. Meanwhile, cook bacon in a large skillet over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until brown and crisp, 5–8 minutes.
Add corn and cook, tossing occasionally, until tender, about 5 minutes. Add Swiss chard leaves; season with salt and pepper. Cook, tossing often, until wilted, about 5 minutes.
Toss in pasta and reserved pasta cooking liquid; cook until sauce coats pasta, about 2 minutes. Serve dolloped with ricotta and topped with basil.

ovar-sponge-cake-teleculinaria.name: Ovar Sponge Cake
instructions: With the mixer, beat the yolks with eggs, sugar and the pinch of salt for about 15 to 20 minutes at medium/ high speed. You should get a bulky, spongy cream. Then wrap the flour and yeast with a spatula.

Bind a shape (23 cm in diameter) with butter and forre with vegetable paper. Put the dough in it and put it in the oven, preheated at 185oC, for about 30 minutes. Remove from the oven and let it cool in shape before deforming.

oven-roasted-horse-mackerel-teleculinari.name: Oven-roasted Horse Mackerel

ingredients.item: Sal e mistura de pimentas q.b.

instructions: Wash the parsley and coriander, dry them with absorbent paper and pinch them. Peel and pinch your garlic teeth. Dawn the fish, wash them and let them run. Season with salt, pepper, lemon juice, olive oil, chopped garlic, blonde, thyme, coriander, parsley, thyme leaves and pepper. Reserve.

Peel and cut the onions to the buds. Put the onion on a tray and the fish on top. Water with the oil and take it in the oven, preheated to 190°C, for about 25 minutes. Follow with tomato migas and serve immediately.

overnight-blueberry-oats.name: Overnight Blueberry Oats
instructions: Place the oats, milk, frozen blueberries, chia seeds, maple syrup, salt, and spirulina in a lidded jar or small bowl. Stir well until fully combined and evenly blue.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or up to 12 hours, until thick and creamy.
Uncover the oat mixture, and spread the yogurt evenly over the top. Set aside.
Microwave the white chocolate chips and coconut oil in a small microwavable bowl on HIGH until smooth and melted, for about 1 minute, stopping to stir every 15 seconds.
Pour the melted white chocolate mixture over the yogurt layer, tilting the jar to coat evenly. Refrigerate for 5–10 minutes, until the shell hardens.
Tap or crack through the magic shell with your spoon, and serve.
Enjoy!

oyster-swiss-chard-gratin-with-country-b.name: Oyster Swiss Chard Gratin With Country Bacon

ingredients.prep: freshly shucked, patted dry, and coarsely chopped

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C.

Brown the bacon in a large sauté pan over medium heat. Add the butter, onion, garlic, and Swiss chard and sauté until the chard is completely wilted. Remove from the heat. Pour the mixture into a colander set in the sink and squeeze out all excess liquid. Reserve.

Return the pan to the stove and add the cream and nutmeg. Bring to a boil over high heat, then decrease the heat to medium-low so the cream does not boil over. Cook the cream until it reduces to 1 cup. Set aside to cool.

In a bowl, combine the chard mixture, cooled cream, and oysters. Mix well and season with salt and pepper. Spoon the mixture into a 3-quart gratin dish. Using the back of a spoon, spread the mixture evenly. In a small bowl, mix together the Parmesan cheese and bread crumbs and sprinkle the topping evenly over the gratin.
Bake until the mixture is bubbling around the sides and the crust is lightly golden brown, about 12 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
